threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You know you're new when you order a bagel and ask for it toasted. The locals just look at you like you've sprouted wings.
Caught a glimpse of a couple arguing over a kebab as we rolled past the Windmill. Only in Brixton, right? They've got a way of turning late night drama into a snack stop.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a group of kids playing soccer in the street while a kebab shop's smoke wafted overhead—it felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab stand with a rotating art exhibit above it. Who knew döner could be so cultured?

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their patience at the BART delays—newbies just complain while we’re already scrolling through NextBus for alternatives.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught that bus this morning packed with folks, even had a guy playing the accordion. Only in SF can rush hour feel like a street performance.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is always buzzing with parties, but locals know it’s the quiet corners and the scent of fresh paella on Sunday that make it special.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newbie by their bewilderment at the local shop owners insisting on greeting them with a hearty 'Hola!' before asking for their order.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You keep trying to pay in cash at the markets, but no one accepts it. This isn't the pub, mate.
Caught the 59 last night; three different people tried to pay in beer cans. Driver just shook his head, while a couple of pigeons nested by the doors.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I spotted the young couple painting a mural on an old garage, turning it into a community art piece that made us all smile.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you see an entire club that’s also a former public swimming pool, where the bass drops are as refreshing as a dive.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell who's local when they won't blink at a BART delay but still freak out over a Mission burrito running out of salsa.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 47 at 5 PM today, and the driver blasted 90s hip-hop. For a moment, it felt less like transit and more like a block party.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the Gaudí sites, but it's really the quirky corners like Carrer de l'Escorial where the real charm lies.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their map app obsession; locals navigate by intuition and landmark memory, not by screens.
